senses.definition: used to refer to someone who has become more comfortable or confident in conversing with people

examples.x: After years of being reserved and quiet, Mark finally broke out of his shell during college.
Ever since joining the drama club, Sarah has really come out of her shell.
It's amazing to see how Lisa has come out of her shell at work.

senses.definition: to be determined or courageous enough to do something dangerous or risky

examples.x: I don't think I have the stomach for horror movies.

senses.definition: to have the necessary will, courage, etc. to get something done

examples.x: Despite her fear of heights, she had the heart to climb the mountain and conquer her phobia.
Even after facing setbacks, she still had the heart to pursue her artistic dreams.
The firefighters displayed incredible bravery and had the heart to enter the burning building to save lives.

senses.definition: to have the necessary conviction or be brave enough to get something done

examples.x: He had the nerve to challenge the status quo and advocate for change in the organization.
I admire her for having the spine to travel solo and explore unfamiliar places.
I don't think I have the guts to go bungee jumping; it's too scary for me.
They had the guts to start a grassroots movement and fight for social justice.

senses.definition: an individual who is willing and ready to work

examples.x: Jane was a willing horse for the school fundraiser, baking dozens of cookies without being asked.
My neighbor is a willing horse, always helping me with yardwork or repairs without complaining.
Thanks for always being willing to cover my shifts last minute, you're a real willing horse around here!

senses.definition: an energetic and eager individual who is willing to work hard

examples.x: John is an eager beaver when it comes to academics.
Sarah is such an eager beaver in the office.
The new intern is a real eager beaver.

senses.definition: used to refer to a person with a very high level of enthusiasm

examples.x: I've never seen anyone as keen as mustard about hiking as Jane.
Tom is a talented musician and when it comes to playing the guitar, he's keen as mustard.

senses.definition: a tough person who is not easily affected by emotions

examples.x: Despite facing criticism and adversity, she remained hard as nails and continued to pursue her artistic vision.
He endured the grueling marathon with sheer determination, proving himself to be tough as nails.
The boxer's training regimen was intense, making him hard as nails in the ring.
The CEO's negotiation skills were legendary; she was as hard as nails and always managed to secure the best deals for her company.
The boxer was hard as nails and never backed down from a fight.

senses.definition: to be competent enough to handle a variety of responsibilities or demanding tasks

word_list.pronunciation: stˈænd ˌɑːn wˈʌnz ˈoʊn tˈuː fˈiːt

senses.definition: to be able to take care of oneself without needing any assistance from others

examples.x: After graduating, she learned to stand on her own two feet.
He stood on his own feet and built a successful career.
She wants to stand on her own feet without relying on her family.
He finally stood on his own two feet after years of support.
The company must stand on its own feet without external funding.

senses.definition: someone who likes being alone and does things without asking for help

examples.x: John has always been a bit of a lone wolf, preferring to work on projects by himself rather than in a team.
Sarah's independent nature and preference for solitude make her a true lone wolf.
The detective suspected that the crime was committed by a lone wolf, as there were no indications of collaboration or involvement of a larger group.

senses.definition: a person who lives life independently and not according to the norms or customs of the society

examples.x: Alex is known as a free spirit in the workplace.
Emily is a true free spirit.

senses.definition: one's ability to remain calm in the face of danger or difficulties

examples.x: Despite the intense pressure, the goalkeeper showed nerves of steel, saving multiple penalty shots during the high-stakes soccer match.
The surgeon displayed nerves of steel as they performed the delicate procedure, remaining calm and focused throughout the intense operation.

senses.definition: a male individual who is exceptional or noteworthy when compared with other men

examples.x: As a captain, James led his troops fearlessly into battle, earning the reputation of a man among men on the battlefield.
His tireless efforts to uplift his community and help those in need have established him as a man among men in philanthropy.
John's unwavering integrity and dedication to his team make him a man among men in the world of professional sports.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is extremely calm and tolerant

examples.x: Despite facing numerous setbacks in his career, John remained patient as Job, never losing hope and continuing to work diligently towards his goals.
Sarah's ability to handle criticism and setbacks with grace and patience is truly remarkable; she's as patient as Job.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is ready and willing to try their best in order to get something done

examples.x: Our company has implemented a lean and mean approach to operations, eliminating unnecessary processes and focusing on efficiency.
The startup operates with a lean and mean team, ensuring everyone has clear roles and responsibilities to achieve their goals.
The team's training regimen is designed to make them lean and mean, maximizing their performance on the field,

senses.definition: used to describe a person who is annoyingly confident

examples.x: Despite the criticism, he stood up for his beliefs, bold as brass, refusing to back down.
He approached the stage, as bold as brass, and delivered a powerful and fearless speech.
She walked into the meeting, as bold as brass, and confidently presented her revolutionary ideas.
The young artist showcased her work, bold as brass, unapologetically challenging traditional norms.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is not worried at all in challenging or stressful situations

examples.x: Despite the intense competition, he kept his cool as a cucumber and delivered a flawless performance.
During the job interview, she answered every question confidently and without hesitation, appearing as cool as a cucumber.
Even though the situation was chaotic, she remained cool as a cucumber, calmly directing everyone and finding a solution.
The goalkeeper remained cool as a cucumber, saving every penalty shot with ease.
I was nervous, but she was cool as a cucumber, guiding me through the process.

word_list.word: (as|) calm as a toad in the sun
word_list.pronunciation: æz kˈɑːm æz ɐ tˈoʊd ɪnðə sˈʌn

senses.definition: used to describe a person who manages to stay calm in the most intense situations

examples.x: After the busy work week, I felt calm as a toad in the sun sleeping in on Saturday morning.
Jane was calm as a toad in the sun on her beach vacation, lounging with a book all afternoon without a care in the world.

senses.definition: (of a person) not easily broken, weakened, or defeated
used to describe someone who refuses to change their opinion or chosen course of action

examples.x: No matter how much evidence I presented, he remained stubborn as a mule, refusing to consider any alternative viewpoints.
She's as stubborn as a mule when it comes to trying new foods, sticking to her familiar favorites without any willingness to explore different flavors.

senses.definition: a person who is strong, resilient, and determined, often in the face of challenging circumstances
